title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The keystone and c5c3 main.go files are nearly identical aside from IDs; consider extracting the controller-runtime manager bootstrap into a shared helper (e.g., in internal/common) to avoid future drift when you change logging, metrics, or probe wiring.
- The operator go.mod files pin many indirect dependencies to unusual future pseudo-versions; it would be more robust to regenerate these via `go mod tidy` against a real proxy or align them with the versions used transitively by k8s.io v0.35.1 to avoid potential resolution problems.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The keystone and c5c3 main.go files are nearly identical aside from IDs; consider extracting the controller-runtime manager bootstrap into a shared helper (e.g., in internal/common) to avoid future drift when you change logging, metrics, or probe wiring.
- The operator go.mod files pin many indirect dependencies to unusual future pseudo-versions; it would be more robust to regenerate these via `go mod tidy` against a real proxy or align them with the versions used transitively by k8s.io v0.35.1 to avoid potential resolution problems.
~~~

</details>

***

resolution_summary: A new shared bootstrap package was created at internal/common/bootstrap/ containing a Run() helper that centralizes controller-runtime manager setup (flag parsing, zap logging, metrics, and health/ready probe wiring) along with a ManagerConfig struct and validation, plus unit tests. The internal/common/.gitkeep placeholder was removed in favor of the new package files, and go.work was updated to alphabetically sort operator entries. However, the diff does not show the keystone and c5c3 main.go files being refactored to actually consume the new bootstrap.Run() helper, nor does it show any go.mod changes addressing the unusual pseudo-version pinning concern from the second review comment.
